etymology.description: original ideogram: tiger with exhaled air -> tiger's roar (號)口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") and 言(言) (yán, "speech") added to emphasize calling -> final form: phonetic 号 (hào, "to wail") + semantic 虎(虎) (hǔ, "tiger")
etymology.interpretation: Originally depicted a tiger exhaling air, signifying its roar. To emphasize the concept of making sounds, later forms incorporated 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") indicating vocal expressions. In the current form, the character combines phonetic 号 (hào, "to wail") with the semantic element 虎(虎) (hǔ, "tiger"), implying the sound made by a tiger. Over time, the meaning was extended to include 'number', possibly due to its phonetic similarity.

examples.chinese: 今天是十月十号(今天是十月十號)。
examples.pinyin: Jīntiān shì shí yuè shí hào.

examples.chinese: 我的电话号码是三八九号(我的電話號碼是三八九號)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de diànhuà hàomǎ shì sān bā jiǔ hào.

examples.chinese: 请到三号台办手续(請到三號台辦手續)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng dào sān hào tái bàn shǒuxù.
examples.english: Please go to counter number three to handle the procedure.
